data.title: As a developer, T12 kudos trigger fires for commented-out code removal in Python, JS/TS, Ruby, and Java projects
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace the Python-anchored regex implementation of T12 with multi-language line-start-anchored regexes that cover Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Ruby, and Java syntax patterns for commented-out code.

**Why**: T12 today only detects removed Python comments (`def`, `class`, `import`, `from`). Ruby developers removing commented-out `def`/`require` lines and JS/TS developers removing `function`/`const`/`import` lines receive zero recognition.

**Scope**:
- IN: Line-start-anchored regex patterns for Python (`def`, `class`, `import`, `from`), JS/TS (`function`, `const`, `let`, `var`, `import`, `export`), Ruby (`def`, `end`, `require`, `require_relative`, `class`, `module`), Java (`public`, `private`, `protected`, `class`, `import`, `void`, `static`); fired when git diff shows removal of lines matching these patterns preceded by comment markers (`#` or `//`)
- OUT: T7, T9, T13, T4; AST-based detection (line-start regex only)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, T12 fires on a Ruby file where a commented-out `def` or `require` line is removed in the diff
  ← Satisfies: "T12 uses line-start-anchored regexes covering Python, JS/TS, Ruby, Java syntax for commented-out code detection"
- When this story is complete, T12 fires on a JS/TS file where a commented-out `function`, `const`, or `import` line is removed in the diff
  ← Satisfies: "T12 uses line-start-anchored regexes covering Python, JS/TS, Ruby, Java syntax for commented-out code detection"
- When this story is complete, T12 does NOT fire on removal of active (non-commented) lines
  ← Satisfies: correctness of detection
- When this story is complete, the TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal (if emitted) conforms to Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) schema; no shared library imported from Kudos Epic 2
  ← Satisfies: "All five triggers emit machine-readable TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for each language's regex patterns (positive and negative cases), including Python (regression), Ruby, JS/TS, and Java

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal format: reference Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) for the required field schema; implement independently
- [Reliability] dispatch_kudos_trigger() from Kudos Epic 1 (3e9b-afee) must be mocked in unit tests
- [Testing] Regex tests must cover both comment markers: `#` (Python, Ruby) and `//` (JS/TS, Java); test with synthetic diff strings, not real git operations

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
